transcript.clean_text: stage seven of parinis took the riders up into the high mountains the only summit finish up at the calder torini for just the second time in the race's history 155 kilometers with one classified climb to come before the final test itself a ride up the valley and a 15 kilometer rise at 7.3 percent snow away did the riders up at the top it was a cold day but thankfully no precipitation a group of 18 riders got up the road hong kong the most dangerous had over five minutes in the general classification and they were never allowed more than a minute and a half megan marco asking that question a rather jovial style of lucrative and once the climb began the break knew that their days were numbered ineos grenadiers and archaic samsic have worked all day to control the breakaway try to set up their various gc leaders adam yates danny martinez and naido quintana second place simon yates was following as well and with only three riders left in the breakaway ten kilometers to go gregor mulberger tried his own luck it was always going to be difficult though by this stage rowan dennis was riding for the race leader primos roglic mateo jorgensen would struggle to hold on on the back he'd be on his way to seeding his white jersey as the best young rider in the race and adam yates started the party at the front his attack with just over seven kilometers to go set the gc pulses racing night or quintana would try to follow him that drew out primoz roglich and danny martinez who was marking adam yates was caught and then danny martinez came over the top just as simon yates was getting on for bike exchange jayco not long though before primogeoglitch decided to attack himself six kilometers remained taking matters into his own hands and swallowing up mulberger the break was done danny martinez was straight on the wheel and simon yates set off in chase dropping his brother but bringing naido quintana with him roglic looks so powerful behind a group involving jack hague as well as maori von savant surviving and racing into the best young leaders jersey we're at about 20 seconds enter the final couple of kilometers and simon yates try this luck they'd all be having a go at each other with nairo quintana continuously distanced but he keep coming back trying to take advantage of the tactical stalemate joao almeida had made his way across as well but by this time we're into the final 300 meters and with 200 to go primoz roglic made his move the yellow jersey with just a few days of racing in his legs at his first stage race of 2022 taking his first victory he's now just one day away from taking another gc title the yellow jersey adding time and bonus seconds to his lead primoz roglich finishing the job ahead of martinez and simon yates quintana coming across in fourth and a good recovery from joao almeida after a poor start to the week roglich asked the question and responding emphatically so roglich beating martinez yates quintana almeida with mcnulty hague adam yates mata and pools all in the top 10 separated by 56 seconds on the day and in the yellow jersey again at the top of the caldechurini primos roglic is one day away from victory of pachinis but he was in this position in 2021 this time he has a 47 second lead simon yates is second danny martinez is third at one minute as pia latour drops to eighth at 256. so can roglich hold on one more stage to go 115 kilometers in the back hills around nice five climbs and the famous calders to finish things off you can catch all the action live or on demand on gcn plus and discovery plus
